verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24δ) is a type I single-pass TM protein with a large lumenal/extracellular GOLD domain and short cytosolic tail, predominantly resident in the early secretory pathway (ER-Golgi) as a cargo receptor. However, a documented minority PM pool exists: TMED10 is reported at the plasma membrane of pancreatic β-cells (insulin secretion), at neuronal presynaptic membranes, and ~0.5% of cellular TMED10 is detectable at the cell surface engaged with misfolded GPI-anchored cargo (PrP*) via the secretory pathway. Its own TM anchor orients the GOLD ectodomain extracellularly when at the PM.
